# Project overview

Wikiwiki keeps local article-first project wikis beside the code, with structured records as the durable source of truth.


Categories: `product`, `overview`, `homepage`

Aliases: `overview`, `what is wikiwiki`

Files: `README.md`, `docs/concepts.md`, `src/core/store.ts`, `src/core/renderer.ts`, `src/core/site.ts`

Wikiwiki is a local-first project wiki system for code repos. It stores durable knowledge as structured JSONL records, renders article-led Markdown into `wiki/`, and builds a static `wiki-site/` that agents and humans can both inspect.

Use it when a repo needs living documentation that stays beside the code rather than in a hosted service or hidden memory layer. Articles are the public wiki pages; concepts, decisions, notes, events, symbols, and links are the evidence ledger behind them.

The CLI stays scriptable and provider-agnostic. Agentic IDEs can use the same deterministic commands to inspect status, capture records, regenerate docs, and close out work without turning the wiki into an opaque AI artifact.
